name: supply-chain-risk-auditor
description: Trustworthyness audit for dependencies — single-maintainer risk, abandoned-repo risk, low-popularity risk, maintainer-identity hygiene, social-engineering resistance. Distinct from security-devsecops (CVE scan at install time): this skill audits the supply side that CVE scanning cannot reach. Run before adding a dependency with non-trivial footprint; quarterly for lockfile-wide review; before committing across releases.

positive_triggers.input: We're considering adding `tiny-graphql-client` for our backend. Before we add it to the lockfile, assess its supply-chain risk.
positive_triggers.reason: Pre-add supply-chain audit on a single dep → matches Use when 'adding a new dependency'

positive_triggers.input: Quarterly review: scan the entire lockfile for any dependency whose maintenance signal has degraded since last review.
positive_triggers.reason: Quarterly cadence lockfile-wide review → matches Use when 'reviewing the lockfile quarterly'

positive_triggers.input: Just heard about the recent npm package-hijack in our ecosystem. Re-audit similar packages in our lockfile to see if we're exposed.
positive_triggers.reason: Post-incident re-audit of similar supply-chain risk class → matches Use when 'after a dependency incident in your ecosystem'

negative_triggers.input: Run npm audit on this dep install
negative_triggers.reason: CVE scan at install time → matches NOT for 'per-install CVE scan' → security-devsecops routes here

negative_triggers.input: Audit who knows our crypto and payment code paths
negative_triggers.reason: Audit of your own codebase's people↔file ownership → matches NOT for 'audit of who knows what in your own codebase' → security-ownership-map routes here

negative_triggers.input: Production just got breached via a compromised dep, what do we do?
negative_triggers.reason: Post-breach incident response → matches NOT for 'production runtime incident' → security-incident-response routes here

behavioral_evals.scenario: Engineer reaches for supply-chain-risk-auditor before adding tiny-graphql-client to the lockfile for the auth-sensitive path.
expected_behavior: §1 inventory: ecosystem=npm, package=tiny-graphql-client, depth=0, used_in=auth/session, security_critical=yes (because session auth)

§2 per-dep 5-axis scoring with concrete evidence: commits-in-last-90d count, unique-authors-in-history, last release date, stars / downloads / dependents, maintainer account created_at, 2FA flag, PGP signatures on tags, SECURITY.md presence
Each axis scoring cites the specific evidence (not just 'MEDIUM')
Aggregate risk score + band per the formula (axis1*2 + axis2*1 + axis3*1 + axis4*2 + axis5*1) / 7
For depth=0 + security_critical=yes, the band escalates one level (any axis = CRITICAL → REJECT)
Output saved at <repo>-supply-chain-risk.md with the dep's row visible at the top of direct deps + Recommendations: REJECT / REVIEW / accept
If risk band is REVIEW or REJECT, the recommendations section lists specific alternatives or fork candidates, not 'consider alternatives'

behavioral_evals.scenario: Engineer runs the audit and gets axis4 (maintainer identity) = CRITICAL because maintainer account was created < 90 days ago. Engineer asks 'is this real?'
expected_behavior: Skill does NOT dismiss the finding — a recently-created account on a dep with thousands of dependents is the canonical npm-hijack pattern
Skill offers context: 'the maintainer could be a legitimate new contributor receiving the project, but combined with the bus-factor-1 axis, the trust signal is HIGH RISK'

Skill recommends: request the previous maintainer announce the transfer on the project page; check if any commit was authored from a new IP; check GitHub for any security advisory opened in the past 30 days; if you proceed, pin + set up Renovate for early-warning
If the dep is security-critical, default recommendation is replace or fork, not just pin
Output reports the finding with severity + the specific evidence (account_created_at timestamp) and the recommended action; second reviewer can verify and act

notes: Source adapted from antigravity supply-chain-risk-auditor. Distinct from security-devsecops (CVE at install time) and security-incident-response (post-breach). Runs before lockfile entry or quarterly for governance review. Methodology reproducibility is mandatory — second reviewer should reach the same scores on the same commands. Scoring matrix documented; per-axis breakdown not just aggregate score.
